summary: We are in full execution mode for the China Going Global report and Intelligent China program launch, with the Knowledge website redesign and Asia Start campaigns also active.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the China Going Global report (joint with IE) targeting a September launch at Singapore events; the team is translating content, identifying alumni case studies, and confirming event partnerships. The Intelligent China program website must launch this week to start driving traffic for the December program, while the Asia Start campaign is live with two parallel routes (AI and traditional). The Knowledge website redesign is in review with two design directions, and we have decided to prioritize Media Minds for social media and to focus SEO efforts on Asia Star and Intelligent China over US programs. Urgent this week: finalize Intelligent China web page, provide feedback on Joker's design, and send updated key messages for the report.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's knowledge website redesign, noting it's similar to current site with menu improvements. Discussed Joseph's alternative design for inspiration, focusing on showing more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed website menu feedback, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from deans and case studies, targeting launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with modules in Beijing, Shanghai/Hangzhou, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on geopolitical tensions affecting European companies in China, with emphasis on de-risking and the role of companies versus governments.

decisions.text: Remove videos as a separate category; integrate them into topics.
Keep knowledge menu centered, school menu at top right.
Show more articles on homepage, with pinned article and latest below.
Bring indices (BCI, BSI) to knowledge homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; US partnerships on hold initially.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; focus on driving traffic starting July.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, incorporating 10 points.
Prioritize Media Minds for social media agency due to small budget and time constraints.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Leeway and op-eds from both deans and possibly alumni.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target to have digital version ready by early September for Singapore events.
Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program is a 12-day in-depth program with three modules, targeting foreigners, starting December 1 in Beijing.
Use the editorial design direction for Knowledge revamp.
Proceed with Flow for design of China Going Global report despite higher cost.
